channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Venezuelan parents watch from home as their son is laid to rest in Texas after drowning
item.description: EAGLE PASS, Texas (AP) — The cries of Liliana Olivero watching from Venezuela through a cellphone pierced through the somber Christian music and gusts of wind as her son's casket was lowered into the ground.
item.dc:creator: VALERIE GONZALEZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Hawks, butterflies and a song for her murdered son help Georgia mom reclaim joy after killings
item.description: Ever since a hawk hovered over a vigil for her murdered son and lifted her spirits, Georgia mother Michele Davis has found joy in unexpected ways.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Martin, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Alabama minor is jailed with adults pre-trial in violation of federal law
item.description: MONTGOMERY, Ala. (AP) — Djovani Jean-Pierre spent his 17th birthday in a small county jail in rural northern Alabama, where he has languished for more than half the school year awaiting trial. On the advice of his 51-year-old cellmate, Jean-Pierre avoids contact with others in the jail.
item.dc:creator: SAFIYAH RIDDLE, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Violent and sudden. What a firing squad execution looked like through my eyes
item.description: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — I've now watched through glass and bars as 11 men were put to death at a South Carolina prison. None of the previous 10 prepared me for watching the firing squad death of Brad Sigmon on Friday night. I might now be unique among U.S.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: A South Carolina man executed by firing squad is the first US prisoner killed this way in 15 years
item.description: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — A South Carolina man who killed his ex-girlfriend’s parents with a baseball bat was executed by firing squad Friday, the first U.S. prisoner in 15 years to die by that method, which he saw as preferable to the electric chair or lethal injection.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Late car payments hit highest level in decades
item.description: Americans are falling behind on their monthly car payments at the highest rate in more than 30 years.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, KREX 5

item.title: A car pulled from a river may tell what happened to an Oregon family of 5 that went missing in 1958
item.description: After two days of dredging, a crane on Friday pulled from the Columbia River a Ford station wagon that officials believe belonged to an Oregon family who disappeared while on a trip 66 years ago to find Christmas greenery.
item.dc:creator: MARTHA BELLISLE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A timeline of how actor Gene Hackman and Betsy Arakawa died at their New Mexico home
item.description: SANTA FE, N.M. (AP) — Actor Gene Hackman died of heart disease a full week after his wife died from hantavirus in their New Mexico home, likely unaware that she was dead because he was in the advanced stages of Alzheimer’s disease, authorities revealed Friday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Police: Colorado woman with 7 kids pointed gun at another driver in road rage incident
item.description: A woman was arrested after she allegedly pointed a gun at another driver in Commerce City on Thursday while seven kids were in the vehicle.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Williams, KRON4

item.title: Homeland Security ends TSA collective bargaining agreement, in effort to dismantle union protections
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Department of Homeland Security said Friday that it is ending the collective bargaining agreement with the tens of thousands of frontline employees at the Transportation Security Administration, marking a major effort to dismantle union protections under the Trump administratio
item.dc:creator: REBECCA SANTANA and MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: A single day of Trump and Musk's cost-cutting campaign remakes huge sections of government
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A series of decisions revealed Friday provided a glimpse of the turmoil engulfing federal agencies since President Donald Trump and Elon Musk launched their campaign of disruption, upending how government functions in ways big and small.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Moon mission goes sideways: Private lunar lander declared dead after botched landing
item.description: A private lunar lander is no longer working after landing sideways in a crater near the moon’s south pole and its mission is over, officials said Friday.
item.dc:creator: By MARCIA DUNN AP Aerospace Writer, Fox 5

item.title: California, other states suing Trump administration over mass firings of probationary federal workers
item.description: California and 19 other states are suing multiple federal agencies, contending President Donald Trump's administration has illegally fired thousands of federal probationary workers.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN WITTE, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: CEO of Jack Daniel's responds after whiskey pulled from Canada's shelves
item.description: The Distilled Spirits Council of the United States applauded President Donald Trump’s decision to suspend tariffs one day after some Canadian provinces started pulling U.S. liquor, including Jack Daniel's, from shelves.
item.dc:creator: Nikki McGee, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Cheetos bags full of meth found at post office
item.description: Two Los Angeles residents have been arrested in southern Indiana after police found pounds of meth hidden inside Cheetos bags at a local post office.
item.dc:creator: Joe Schroeder, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Elementary student found unharmed after escaping school
item.description: " I just jumped out of the car and tried to grab him to make sure that there was nobody that actually would hit him," Kohler said.
item.dc:creator: Cheyenne Pagan, NewsNation

item.title: South Carolina convict inches closer to first US death by firing squad in 15 years
item.description: A South Carolina convict who brutally killed his ex-girlfriend's parents with a baseball bat in 2001 is scheduled to be executed by firing squad on Friday – the first execution of its kind in U.S. in 15 years.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump signs executive order creating bitcoin reserve
item.description: Trump, who once called crypto a "scam," has drastically changed his tune on the subject and promised on the campaign trail to make the U.S. the "crypto capital of the world.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Pope Francis hits 3-week mark of hospitalization
item.description: Francis offered a first public sign of just how weak he is on Thursday by recording an audio message that was broadcast to the faithful in St. Peter’s Square.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Alarming number of Americans scammed out of life savings have one thing in common, prompting lawmaker response
item.description: With romance scams on the rise, a bipartisan group of lawmakers is introducing new legislation to hold fraudsters accountable for targeting Americans' savings.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Judge says plans to release a woman in Slender Man case can go forward
item.description: WAUKESHA, Wis. (AP) — A Wisconsin woman who nearly killed her classmate years ago to please horror character Slender Man can be released from a psychiatric hospital as planned, a judge decided Thursday, rejecting state health officials’ last-minute attempt to keep her committed.
item.dc:creator: TODD RICHMOND, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: 90s band Spin Doctors' equipment stolen from Pennsylvania storage unit
item.description: Pennsylvania State Police stated that on February 22, troopers began investigating the theft of music equipment from a storage unit at D.M.D Mini Storage in Penn Township, Snyder County.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Muniz, Fox 5

item.title: Experts and recovery teams hope to reach site of Alaska avalanche that buried 3 skiers
item.description: GIRDWOOD, Alaska (AP) — Authorities in Alaska said they would attempt Thursday to reach the site of an avalanche that trapped three skiers, who were believed to have died after being buried in snow.
item.dc:creator: MARK THIESSEN and BECKY BOHRER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Bryan Kohberger case: Idaho court releases surviving roommates' text messages from night of student murders
item.description: Text messages between two surviving roommates of four university students killed in a home invasion stabbing have been revealed for the first time.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: CDC rehiring some laid-off workers
item.description: Administration officials told the CDC last month that nearly 1,300 of its probationary employees would be cut, but only approximately 700 to 750 employees ended up receiving termination notices.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, WGN 9

item.title: Why Christians use eggs in Easter celebrations
item.description: With egg prices up 53% from a year ago, some Christians may be considering skipping using eggs in their Easter celebrations this year.
item.dc:creator: Karen Hendren, NewsNation

item.title: President Trump delays some tariffs on Mexico and Canada
item.description: President Donald Trump Thursday paused the 25% tariffs on Canada and Mexico that started earlier this week, with plans to restart them in April.
item.dc:creator: Maddie Biertempfel, KTLA 5

item.title: Southwest plane forced to go back to gate as passenger strips naked: reports
item.description: A Southwest passenger forced a plane bound for Phoenix from Houston this week to return to the gate after she took off all of her clothes and started harassing others.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Car being pulled from an Oregon river might have belonged to a family that vanished in 1958
item.description: Authorities said Thursday that they planned to pull a station wagon from an Oregon river believed to have belonged to a family of five that disappeared nearly 70 years ago while out searching for Christmas greenery.
item.dc:creator: MARTHA BELLISLE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump says he will 'probably' extend TikTok ban deadline if deal isn't reached in time
item.description: President Trump on Thursday suggested he would consider extending the deadline for a ban on TikTok for a second time if a deal isn't reached, stating there is "a lot of interest" in the video-sharing platform.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, KRON4

item.title: Men accused of stealing and reselling StubHub tickets for $635K: DA
item.description: Two men accused of stealing and reselling hundreds of StubHub tickets were arrested and arraigned Monday, according to Queens District Attorney Melinda Katz.
item.dc:creator: Ben Mitchell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: No prison time for man who bashed, disfigured mother’s face in front of child
item.description: Trevor Lee Colombano, 38, faced up to nine years in state prison in addition to 18 months in county jail after pleading no contest to assault with a deadly weapon, sexual battery and mayhem.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Mense, NewsNation

item.title: Andrew Tate says he plans to live in Florida despite 'hee haw' over return to US soil amid overseas charges
item.description: Andrew Tate says he plans to buy a Florida penthouse and live in the United States as he awaits trial in Romania on sex trafficking and other charges.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump signs tariff exemptions for certain imports from Canada, Mexico
item.description: President Trump on Thursday signed off on tariff exemptions for imports from Canada and Mexico that are covered under a 2020 North American trade agreement, marking the latest reversal of duties imposed earlier this week. Under the orders, goods compliant with the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Maine student passes out, 11 taken to hospital as cause of illnesses remains unknown
item.description: The all-clear has been given to return to an elementary school in Maine after 11 students were taken to hospitals for reported respiratory illnesses.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Oregon man accused of throwing Molotov cocktails, shooting at Tesla dealership
item.description: Adam Matthew Lansky, 41, has been arraigned on federal charges after being accused of attempting to destroy property at a Tesla dealership twice.
item.dc:creator: Aimee Plante, Fox 5

item.title: Man treated 'like a dog' before murder in upstate New York, prosecutor says
item.description: CANANDAIGUA, N.Y. (AP) — Seven people accused of killing a 24-year-old man in upstate New York subjected him to weeks of torture that included sexual assault, forcing him to eat feces and pouring bleach on him, prosecutors said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4
